reasoning: The question asks if Anand's ranking will remain at least as high as his October 2024 ranking (estimated at rank 13, Elo 2743). As an inactive player his rating is highly stable. However, there are numerous active top players just 5-6 points below him (MVL, Niemann, Dominguez, Ding Liren) and one just 1 point below him (Mamedyarov). Major events occurred in October 2024 (US Championship, European Club Cup, WR Chess Masters). MVL had a strong performance in October and likely gained enough points to surpass Anand's 2743. Meanwhile, the players slightly above Anand (So, Keymer, Abdusattorov) are less likely to have dropped the 8-12 points necessary to fall below him. Even if Anand played in WR Masters, it's highly likely he either lost points or stayed stagnant, making him vulnerable to being surpassed by younger/more active players. Because the density of players immediately below him is greater than above him, a relative rank drop to 14 or 15 is the most probable outcome for the November, December, and January lists. I start my probability under 50% reflecting this pressure, and regress toward 0.50 as the timeline extending into 2025 increases variance.
